content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10650427/", "source_title": "Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on Photovoltaic Module Durability", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.8, "overall_relevance": 0.82, "year": 2023, "authors": [], "venue": "PMC / Polymers", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "The optimal gel content range for EVA encapsulant stability was identified as 84-90%, with samples below 70% gel content showing low chemical and optical stability, weak adhesion strength, and EVA flowing, while laminates over 92% gel content were more prone to yellowing and less stable in adhesion after stress testing.", "direct_quote": "laminates stability increases with increasing curing level to an upper limit, beyond which leading to the formation of voids, reduced transmittance stability, discoloration, and unstable interfaces.
